pi_items.source: Task description and coordinate matching via Wikipedia/Web search for Atacama Large Millimeter Array
Wikipedia API for Atacama Large Millimeter Array (-23.0193 rounded to -23.02)
Wikipedia API for Atacama Large Millimeter Array (-67.7532 rounded to -67.75)
Calculated via Python math.radians(-23.02) and verified against verifier.py ALMA_LAT_RAD constant
Calculated via Python math.radians(-67.75) and verified against verifier.py ALMA_LON_RAD constant
Confirmed via web search (airmass.org) that ALMA is exactly at 5,058 meters altitude
Web search confirmed 2025-03-23 is the precise real-world date of Saturn's next ring plane crossing. Earlier dates trace the gradual closing of the rings.
Confirmed by analyzing verifier.py and export_result.sh parsing logic
Confirmed by export_result.sh ARTICLE_FILE variable and verifier.py checks
Confirmed by verifier.py logic checking for >= 3 new screenshots

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring Stellarium to simulate observations from the Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA) at its exact real-world coordinates (latitude -23.02°, longitude -67.75°, altitude 5058m). The observation dates culmination is not arbitrary; March 23, 2025 is the exact verified astronomical date of Saturn's next ring plane crossing, when the rings will appear edge-on from Earth. Output validation checks for exactly 3 screenshots in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium and the presence of specific keywords in /home/ga/Desktop/saturn_rings_article.txt, with both atmosphere and landscape rendering disabled in the configuration.
